original_text:
### [IMPORTANT] Icon Callout Box Rule A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge** � the edge runs through the badge's center so it reads as half-on / half-off the box. Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). **Layout (explicit):** - **Container** ? the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`). The blue box itself is **inset from the left by half the badge width** so the badge's outer half has room � this is the "box gets a little smaller on the left" step. Everything else stays full content width. - **Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape. Vertically sized to its text with generous padding; the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`). - **Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge. - **Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), **vertically centered** on the box and **horizontally centered on the box's left edge** (the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background). **Geometry (the part that makes it exact):** - Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside). - Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge** (badge `left:0` inside a `padding-left:40px` wrapper ? badge center `40px` = box edge). Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**. - Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height). **Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order � large on **top-left + bottom-right**, near-point on **top-right + bottom-left**, a **5:1 ratio**. The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal. **Drop shadow (mandatory):** the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box � e.g. `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity). This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on. ## Palette reference | Token | HEX | Use here | |---|---|---| | Brand Blue | `#00529b` | Headline text + icon stroke | | Light Blue | `#E6F0F9` | Box fill | | White | `#ffffff` | Icon badge fill | | Dark Navy | `#011E45` | Drop-shadow color (low opacity) | ## MJML ```xml
These improvements occurred as early as four weeks after starting treatment, and they continued getting better through the end of the 52-week study.
``` ## Email fallback caveats (important) The 50/50 overhang relies on `position:absolute`, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores** � there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across **all** clients, the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG** and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical. ## Scaling note Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`. If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

relations.note: Corner radius mandate is a locked sub-spec of the overall box/badge composition.
Shadow mandate is a locked sub-spec of the overall box/badge composition.
Fallback guidance addresses the Outlook failure mode of the absolute-positioning mechanism defined in the composition rule.

White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)

Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px`

Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).

rules.intent: Preserve the exact half-on/half-off badge-on-edge composition that distinguishes the icon callout as a proof-point device.
rules.rule_text: Icon Callout Box: a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) accent-shape box holding a short Brand Blue (#00529b) headline, paired with a White (#ffffff) icon badge that straddles the box's left edge so the edge passes through the badge's exact center (50% on box, 50% on page background). Used for proof-point statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). The box sits on the page/section background (usually White #ffffff) and is inset from the left by half the badge width (W/2 = 40px); everything else stays full content width. Box left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap (W/2 + 24px = 64px). Text is Brand Blue, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px/28px line-height, left-aligned, and vertically centered — copy must never slide under the badge. The badge is a White rounded square holding a Brand Blue-stroke line icon (80px badge, 48px inner icon), vertically centered on the box and horizontally centered on the box's left edge, achieved via absolute positioning (badge left:0 inside a padding-left:40px wrapper) and vertical centering via top:50% + margin-top:-40px (half the badge height). If badge size changes, the relationships must hold: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = -H/2.
rules.snippets: <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;"> ... <td style="background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;"> ... <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18);...">

border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR)
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR).
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Lock the signature asymmetric accent-shape geometry so the device stays visually consistent across every instance.
rules.rule_text: Icon callout corner radii are mandatory and never change: the box uses border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio), and the white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale with border-radius 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as a distinct layered element rather than flat artwork.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow (box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18) — Dark Navy at 18% opacity) on every instance so it visually lifts off the blue box; omitting it makes the badge look flat and pasted-on.

pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG

ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

rules.intent: Manage the known Outlook rendering limitation for the badge-on-edge device without compromising brand fidelity elsewhere.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 badge-on-edge overhang relies on position:absolute, which Outlook desktop (Word rendering engine) ignores — there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that isn't feasible, ship the live absolute-positioned version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) — every other spec (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, Brand Blue 800 text, drop shadow) must stay identical.
